history: The client has recently completed an undergraduate degree and transitioned into the workforce. They came out to their parents within the last two and a half years, which was met with negative reactions, leading to increased distance. The client moved to New York for education and explored their identity away from family influence. They have a strained relationship with their mother, who is focused on practical concerns rather than emotional support, and tension with a sister who is envious of the client's independence and achievements.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
intermediate_belief: If I disclose personal matters to my parents, then they will react negatively, and I will feel rejected.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I open up about my breakup, it will only confirm that I am not accepted by my family.
coping_strategies: The client uses mathematics as a form of escapism from emotionally charged aspects of their life. They avoid disclosing personal matters to maintain autonomy and control over their narrative.
situation: Deciding whether to disclose a recent breakup to parents.
auto_thought: If I tell them about the breakup, they'll just judge me again.
behavior: The client avoids discussing the breakup with their parents and instead focuses on their work and mathematical interests.
